title: L-glutamine in swine diets
abstract: A swine feed may include a non-antibiotic immunomodulator, for example L-glutamine. The use of such a diet may include feeding it to piglets post-weaning and transportation to alleviate some of the negative effects of such stressors on pig production and welfare. Use of L-glutamine in the piglets' diet may have several beneficial effects, such as the reduction of enteric disease and faster weight gain. The use of L-glutamine may be a cost-effective replacement for traditional dietary antibiotics in swine production.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: Claim 1: A composition of swine feed comprising a therapeutically effective amount of a non-antibiotic immunomodulator. Claim 2: The composition of claim 1, wherein the non-antibiotic immunomodulator is also a growth promotant. Claim 3: The composition of claim 1, wherein the non-antibiotic immunomodulator is L- glutamine. Claim 4: The composition of claim 1, wherein the non-antibiotic immunomodulator is present in an amount of about 0.10% - 1.0% (w/w) as compared to the total amount of the swine feed. Claim 5: The composition of claim 4, wherein the non-antibiotic immunomodulator is present in an amount of about 0.20% - 0.60% as compared to the total amount of the swine feed. Claim 6: A method of decreasing negative effects of a stressor on swine, the method comprising: providing the swine with a first swine feed, the first swine feed comprising a therapeutically effective amount of a non-antibiotic immunomodulator, 

 wherein the swine is provided with the first swine feed during a period before, during, or after the stressor is introduced to the swine. Claim 7: The method of claim 6, wherein the non- antibiotic immunomodulator is also a growth promotant. Claim 8: The method of claim 6, wherein the non- antibiotic immunomodulator is L- glutamine. Claim 9: The method of claim 6, wherein the stressor is one of weaning and transportation. Claim 10: The method of claim 6, wherein the swine is provided with the first swine feed during a period of at least 14 days after the stressor is introduced. Claim 11: The method of claim 6, wherein the negative effects are the incidence of enteric disease in the swine. Claim 12: The method of claim 6, further comprising providing the swine with a second swine feed, wherein the second swine feed does not contain the non-antibiotic immunomodulator, and 

 wherein the swine is provided with the first swine feed during a treatment period, and after the treatment period elapses the swine is provided with the second swine feed during a growth period. Claim 13: The method of claim 12, wherein the treatment period is about 14 days in length. * * * *
